title: Earth Day 2026: 'Our Power, Our Planet' — Global Rally Around Renewable Energy
detail: Earth Day 2026 on April 22 adopted the theme 'Our Power, Our Planet,' centering the global environmental movement on urgent acceleration of renewable energy. Billions of people in 193+ countries participated in events, educational programs, and action campaigns. The Earth Day Network called for global renewable energy generation to triple by 2030 and highlighted that solar and wind are now the cheapest forms of new electricity generation in most markets. Earth Day 2026 arrived amid heightened climate urgency, with oil prices up 60% and gas prices 70% due to the Iran war, driving record purchases of EVs, heat pumps, and rooftop solar across Europe as households sought energy independence. The 56th Earth Day underscored the convergence of energy security and climate action as twin drivers of the clean energy transition.

media.caption: Earth Day 2026 celebrations worldwide centered on the theme 'Our Power, Our Planet' and the clean energy transition

title: EU Unveils AccelerateEU: €660B/yr Clean Energy Plan to End Fossil Dependence
detail: On April 22 — Earth Day — European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen presented AccelerateEU, a comprehensive plan to address rising energy costs from the Iran conflict and accelerate Europe's clean energy transition. The plan targets €660 billion per year in clean energy investment through 2030, coordinates gas storage filling across 27 member states, establishes a new Fuel Price Observatory to track supply and shortages, and commits to an Electrification Action Plan by summer 2026 with ambitious electrification targets for industry, transport, and buildings. EU fossil fuel import costs have risen €24 billion since the Iran conflict began, with oil up 60% and gas 70%. Energy Commissioner Dan Jorgensen stated Europe must 'get rid of dependency on gas as fast as possible.' The plan directly accelerates the European Green Deal, using the energy security crisis as a catalyst to turbocharge the clean transition.

media.caption: EU AccelerateEU plan announced April 22, 2026, targeting €660B/yr in clean energy investment to cut fossil fuel dependence
